old_claim.note: Mobile Robot Navigation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Warehouse AMRs require mobile robots that can move inventory through the facility under navigation and fleet-control software.

source_shape.source_locator: Mobile-Robot-Enabled Smart Warehouses system description of Kiva using hundreds of mobile robots and control software for storing, moving, and sorting inventory.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes Kiva smart warehouses as mobile-robot-enabled fulfillment systems using many mobile robots plus control software to move inventory.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the node is explicitly autonomous mobile warehouse robots, a mobile-robot navigation and control capability is not merely contextual; it is the defining technical dependency.

ontology_before: Warehouse AMRs treated mobile robot navigation as one optional enabler among broad logistics software dependencies.
ontology_after: Warehouse AMRs require mobile robot navigation while WMS remains an operational scaling dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: warehouse_autonomous_mobile_robots -> mobile_robot_navigation is now a required, source-backed edge.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to non-mobile conveyor or fixed automation systems.
A warehouse robotics architecture used no autonomous or guided mobile navigation while still fitting this node's scope.
The source were used to claim all WMS or supply-chain software is a hard prerequisite.
short_reason: Autonomous mobile warehouse robots are defined by mobile navigation and fleet-control capability.
